title: centaurea baksanica
intro: Centaurea baksanica, also known as Baksan knapweed, is a rare and unique plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae), characterized by its distinct morphology and distribution in the mountainous regions of Turkey. Traditionally, Centaurea baksanica has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. Centaurea baksanica is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or dried extracts, which are consumed for their therapeutic properties, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential applications and optimal usage.
